mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following PAYEMS increases are not positive in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% restricts credit availability and raises equity discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment below 4.0% combined with a month-over-month rise in consumer prices indicates an overheating economy that forces abrupt Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of UNRATE below 4.0% and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month declines in housing starts transmit restrictive policy effects into interest-rate-sensitive sectors, predicting negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month HOUST declines during FEDFUNDS > 3.0% regimes coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production signal near-term earnings momentum and capacity utilization gains, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO increases are not positive in at least 53% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month consumer price inflation exceeding 0.3% erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of monetary restraint, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month-over-month over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer spending momentum, driving positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following RSAFS growth are not positive in at least 53% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over two consecutive months signals deteriorating household income and recession risk, generating negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over two consecutive months.
